articles.title: Xbox shouldn't close Ninja Theory, Compulsion, and Double Fine and should never have bought them
articles.summary: Microsoft's acquisition of Double Fine, Ninja Theory, and Compulsion Games has raised concerns that the company may be considering closing these studios or selling them off to other developers. This decision would mark another example of Microsoft's poor record in managing its game development studios, which have seen several high-profile closures in recent years. The studio at risk is Obsidian Entertainment, which has struggled with underperforming games such as Avowed and The Outer Worlds 2. While some may view the Xbox group's mass redundancies as a natural consequence of business decisions, it's worth noting that the company's track record suggests that this is not an isolated incident.
